# Send Streaming Messages

**Source:** https://open.seatalk.io/docs/send-streaming-message

### API Description

Use these APIs to send a streaming message that displays content progressively — similar to a typewriter effect. Streaming is supported in 1-on-1 chats and in group chats.

![wDQ7JOqFofj1AkRVJkhJUA+nIiSq5kizZki75kjAZkzI5kzRZkzZ5kzhAmZM6uZM82ZM++ZNAGZRCOZREWZRGeZRImZRKuZRMqZP605STGFtQOZVUWZVWeZVYmZVauZVY9pRcCSL86DYBAQA7](data:image/gif;base64,)

Supported message types:

- Text messages (with or without Markdown formatting)

- Interactive messages

Note:

- To call these APIs, your app must enable the bot capability and have an Online status. See more at Quickly build a Bot.

- Requires SeaTalk App version 3.67 or later. Recipients on earlier versions will see only the final message after the stream is closed.

- No additional permission required. 1-on-1 streaming uses the Send Message to Bot User permission; group chat streaming uses the Send Message to Group Chat permission.

Limits:

- Time limit: The interval between consecutive `update_stream` calls must not exceed 30 seconds. If exceeded, the stream will be automatically terminated.

- Length limit: The total text content across all fragments must not exceed 4096 characters. Requests that exceed this limit will fail.

- Termination: Once a stream is terminated (by `finish: true`, timeout, or error), all subsequent requests referencing that `stream_id` will be rejected.

- Call frequency: Avoid calling `update_stream` for every character generated. Use a buffer and call approximately every 200ms for optimal efficiency and streaming experience.

How streaming works:

- Call init_stream — sends a placeholder message to the chat and returns a stream_id

- Call update_stream repeatedly as content is generated — pass the full cumulative content each time, and increment seq by 1 starting from 1

- Call update_stream with finish: true — closes the stream and persists the final content as the permanent message

Important: Each update_stream call must contain the full content from the beginning of the stream, not just the new fragment. The client renders progress by displaying the latest snapshot received.

Request Method: POST

### Initialize a Stream

Sends a placeholder message to the chat and returns a stream_id for subsequent updates.

#### Endpoints

| | Endpoint |
| --- | --- |
| 1-on-1 | https://openapi.seatalk.io/messaging/v2/single_chat/init_stream |
| Group chat | https://openapi.seatalk.io/messaging/v2/group_chat/init_stream |

#### Request Parameters

Header

| Parameter | Type | Mandatory | Description | Sample |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Authorization | string | Yes | Obtained through the Get App Access Token API | Bearer c8bda0f77ef940c5bea9f23b2d7fc0d8 |
| Content-Type | string | Yes | Request header format | application/json |

Body

| Parameter | Type | Mandatory | Description | Sample |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| employee_code | string | Yes (1-on-1) | The employee_code of the target user | "abcdef" |
| group_id | string | Yes (group) | The group chat ID | "abcdef" |
| message | object | Yes | The message to initialize as a stream | - |
| thread_id | string | No | Thread ID to send the message as a thread reply. To start a new thread, provide the root message ID. | "om_67890" |
| quoted_message_id | string | No | Message ID to quote and reply to. Group chat only. | "om_12345" |

Message Object

| Parameter | Type | Mandatory | Description | Sample |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| tag | string | Yes | Message type: "text" or "interactive_message" | "text" |
| text | object | Conditional | Required when tag is "text" | - |
| ∟ format | int | No | 1: Markdown (default), 2: Plain text | 1 |
| ∟ content | string | Yes | Initial message content | "Thinking..." |
| interactive_message | object | Conditional | Required when tag is "interactive_message" | - |
| ∟ elements | array | Yes | Card elements. See [Build a Card]. | - |

#### Request Samples

// 1-on-1, text

```json
{
"employee_code": "abcdefgh",
"message": {
"tag": "text",
"text": {
"format": 1,
"content": "Thinking..."
},
"thread_id": "hjsatc"
}
}
```

// Group chat, text (thread_id required)

```json
{
"group_id": "abcdefgh",
"message": {
"tag": "text",
"text": {
"format": 1,
"content": "Thinking..."
},
"quoted_message_id": "bcdef",
"thread_id": "hjsatc"
}
}
```

// Interactive message

```json
{
"employee_code": "abcdefgh",
"message": {
"tag": "interactive_message",
"interactive_message": {
"elements": [
{
"element_type": "title",
"title": {
"text": "Interactive Message Title"
}
},
{
"element_type": "description",
"description": {
"format": 1,
"text": "Loading..."
}
}
]
}
}
}
```

#### Response

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| code | int | Refer to Error Code for explanations |
| stream_id | string | Pass this to all subsequent update_stream calls |

```json
{
"code": 0,
"stream_id": "abc"
}
```

### Push a Stream Update

Pushes content to an active stream. Call repeatedly as content is generated; set finish: true on the final call to close the stream.

#### Endpoints

| | Endpoint |
| --- | --- |
| 1-on-1 | https://openapi.seatalk.io/messaging/v2/single_chat/update_stream |
| Group chat | https://openapi.seatalk.io/messaging/v2/group_chat/update_stream |

#### Request Parameters

Header

| Parameter | Type | Mandatory | Description | Sample |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Authorization | string | Yes | Obtained through the Get App Access Token API | Bearer c8bda0f77ef940c5bea9f23b2d7fc0d8 |
| Content-Type | string | Yes | Request header format | application/json |

Body

| Parameter | Type | Mandatory | Description | Sample |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| employee_code | string | Yes (1-on-1) | The employee_code of the target user | "abcdef" |
| group_id | string | Yes (group) | The group chat ID | "abcdef" |
| stream_id | string | Yes | The stream ID returned by init_stream | "stream_123" |
| seq | int64 | Yes | Sequence number. Start from 1, increment by 1 on each call. Used by the server to handle out-of-order delivery. | 1, 2, 3... |
| finish | bool | No | Set to true on the final call to close the stream and persist the message. Default: false | true |
| message | object | Yes | The full cumulative content from the beginning of the stream | - |

Message Object

| Parameter | Type | Mandatory | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| text | object | Conditional | Required when the stream was initialized with tag: "text" |
| ∟ format | int | No | 1: Markdown (default), 2: Plain text |
| ∟ content | string | Yes | Full content from the start of the stream, including this update |
| interactive_message | object | Conditional | Required when the stream was initialized with tag: "interactive_message" |
| ∟ elements | array | Yes | Card elements. See [Build a Card]. |

#### Request Samples

// Intermediate update

```json
{
"employee_code": "abcdefgh",
"stream_id": "abc",
"seq": 3,
"finish": false,
"message": {
"text": {
"format": 1,
"content": "The answer is 42. Here is"
}
}
}
```

// Final update — closes the stream

```json
{
"employee_code": "abcdefgh",
"stream_id": "abc",
"seq": 10,
"finish": true,
"message": {
"text": {
"format": 1,
"content": "The answer is 42. Here is the full explanation."
}
}
}
```

// Interactive message update

```json
{
"employee_code": "abcdefgh",
"stream_id": "abc",
"seq": 2,
"finish": false,
"message": {
"interactive_message": {
"elements": [
{
"element_type": "title",
"title": {
"text": "Interactive Message Title"
}
},
{
"element_type": "description",
"description": {
"format": 1,
"text": "Updated content..."
}
}
]
}
}
}
```

#### Response

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| code | int | Refer to Error Code for explanations |

```json
{
"code": 0
}
```
